title: polygala vulgaris subsp. pawlowskii
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. pawlowskii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae, commonly known as the common or purple milkwort. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots of Polygala vulgaris subsp. pawlowskii are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and fatigue.
